Title: The wheat Lr34 multipathogen resistance gene confers resistance to anthracnose and rust in sorghum

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native wheat Lr34res promoter (~2.4 kb), inducible/pathogen-responsive in sorghum
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lr34res is a useful transgene for improving sorghum resistance while enhancing pathogen-induced flavonoid phytoalexin accumulation. A low-expression/single-copy deployment appears preferable to reduce yield penalty. The paper specifically recommends exploring pathogen-inducible promoters to retain strong disease protection while minimizing reproductive penalties from high constitutive expression in adult plants.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression of wheat Lr34res in sorghum increases pathogen-induced flavonoid phytoalexin biosynthetic gene expression and elevates 3-deoxyanthocyanidin metabolites, especially luteolinidin, while conferring resistance to rust and anthracnose.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable sorghum transformation with genomic Lr34res; transgene copy-number and expression analysis; pathogen infection assays with Puccinia purpurea and Colletotrichum sublineolum; qRT-PCR of pathway genes; LC-MS metabolite profiling of phytoalexins; fungal biomass quantification; phenotypic scoring.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pathogen inoculation with Puccinia purpurea or Colletotrichum sublineolum; rust assays at 20°C and high humidity; anthracnose mesocotyl infection; post-inoculation time course 24–96 h.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In transgenic sorghum, Lr34res transcripts were highest in penultimate adult leaves of high-copy lines and were induced within 24 h after P. purpurea inoculation, then declined by 48 hpi.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In wheat, Lr34res is known for adult plant resistance and can show elevated seedling expression under low temperature in rust-infected plants, as discussed in the paper.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SbFNSII; SbFNR; SbDFR3 (functional association through enhanced induction, not direct physical interaction)
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolite profiling by LC-MS; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Lr34res encodes an ABC transporter rather than a biosynthetic enzyme. In sorghum it acts across a wide taxonomic boundary and is associated with stronger pathogen-induced pigmentation and elevated phytoalexin accumulation. High-expression multicopy lines showed near immunity to rust; single-copy lines showed partial/slow-rusting resistance.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High-expression multicopy lines had reduced vigour, smaller panicles, reduced panicle weight, reduced peduncle diameter, and lower 100-seed weight. Single-copy low-expression lines had much lower fitness cost.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Wheat Lr34res functions in transgenic sorghum and enhances resistance to sorghum rust and anthracnose. 2. Lr34res expression parallels pathogen-induced leaf pigmentation and stronger induction of sorghum flavonoid phytoalexin pathway genes SbFNSII, SbFNR and SbDFR3. 3. Lr34res is associated with increased 3-deoxyanthocyanidin accumulation, especially luteolinidin, but high expression causes yield penalties.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Infected sorghum mesocotyls and pathogen-challenged leaf epidermal sites/pigmented spots
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antifungal defense metabolites associated with restricting pathogen spread and contributing to resistance against rust and anthracnose.

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native sorghum Sb01g016775 promoter; constitutive/basal with negative response to rust inoculation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Simple coding-sequence editing of the sorghum ortholog to mimic the two key LR34RES amino-acid changes is insufficient. The paper suggests that any future engineering would likely need additional sequence changes, including pathogen-responsive regulatory sequences beyond the coding region.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Modified sorghum Sb01g016775 (ΔF525, Y613H) failed to confer rust resistance like wheat Lr34res; the native sorghum ortholog was negatively responsive to pathogen inoculation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-directed mutagenesis of Sb01g016775; stable transformation; transcript confirmation; P. purpurea infection phenotyping; comparative gene-expression analysis under mock and rust inoculation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Puccinia purpurea inoculation; 24 hpi expression comparison; disease scoring at 14 dpi
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Sb01g016775 showed weak negative responsiveness to P. purpurea inoculation in sorghum.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Same as applied species, because this gene is endogenous to sorghum.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Sb01g016775 is an Lr34 ortholog sharing ~75% protein identity with wheat LR34. Altering the two key amino acids to mimic LR34RES did not reproduce resistance, indicating additional sequence/regulatory requirements.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sorghum Sb01g016775 is the endogenous ortholog of wheat Lr34. 2. Editing the two critical residues to mimic LR34RES did not confer rust resistance. 3. Its pathogen expression response differs from wheat Lr34res, supporting the importance of broader sequence/regulatory context.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antifungal defense metabolites in sorghum; this gene was tested in relation to the resistance/phytoalexin-associated system but no positive functional effect was shown.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous sorghum promoter; inducible by pathogen infection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: SbFNSII is a candidate pathway engineering target to increase flavone-related defense metabolites, but in this paper its induction is mainly a downstream response enhanced by Lr34res rather than a directly engineered locus.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts flavanones such as naringenin/eriodictyol into flavones by introducing the C2–C3 double bond.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SbFNSII expression was strongly induced after rust inoculation, and induction was higher in high-Lr34res lines, consistent with enhanced flavonoid phytoalexin/flavone production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time-course after P. purpurea inoculation; association with pathogen-induced pigmentation and LC-MS metabolite profiling context.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms branch products related to defense flavones and contributes to phytoalexin-associated flavonoid output
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: P. purpurea infection; peak induction at 24 hpi
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Pathogen inducible in sorghum leaves; transcript levels increased at 24 hpi and declined by 48 hpi; strongest in high-Lr34res genotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Same as applied species, endogenous sorghum gene.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as a cytochrome P450 pathogen-inducible gene. The study quantified transcript induction but did not directly manipulate SbFNSII.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Flavone branch of sorghum flavonoid phytoalexin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbFNSII is a pathogen-inducible sorghum flavonoid pathway gene. 2. Its expression peaks at 24 hpi after rust inoculation and is much higher in strongly expressing Lr34res lines. 3. This supports enhanced flavonoid defense metabolism in Lr34res plants.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Flavones and related flavonoid phytoalexin-associated metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Pathogen-infected sorghum leaves and mesocotyl tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-associated metabolites contributing to antifungal responses.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous sorghum promoter; inducible by pathogen infection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: SbFNR is a strong candidate marker or engineering target for increasing sorghum 3-deoxyanthocyanidin-based defense metabolites, although this paper only measured its induced expression downstream of Lr34res.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces flavanones at the 4-position to produce flavan-4-ols, key intermediates toward 3-deoxyanthocyanidins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SbFNR was massively induced by rust infection, especially in high-Lr34res lines, consistent with enhanced flux to 3-deoxyanthocyanidin phytoalexins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time-course after P. purpurea inoculation, interpreted together with metabolite accumulation data and pathway schematic.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies direct precursor pool for 3-deoxyanthocyanidin phytoalexins
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: P. purpurea infection; peak induction at 24 hpi
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly pathogen inducible in sorghum leaves, peaking at 24 hpi and declining at 48 hpi; >140-fold induction in Lr34-5 at peak.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Same as applied species, endogenous sorghum gene.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream to downstream committed defense branch step
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted as one of the pathway genes whose induction best paralleled strong Lr34res expression and pigmentation.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NADPH-dependent reductase, short-chain dehydrogenase/reductase-like flavonoid enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbFNR is a key sorghum 3-deoxyanthocyanidin pathway enzyme gene monitored in this study. 2. Its transcript induction is markedly enhanced in Lr34res lines after rust infection. 3. The expression pattern supports elevated synthesis of antifungal 3-deoxyanthocyanidin phytoalexins.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Infected sorghum mesocotyls and leaf infection sites
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antifungal defense metabolites that restrict pathogen spread.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous sorghum promoter; inducible by pathogen infection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: SbDFR3 can be considered for metabolic engineering of sorghum defense flavonoids, but in this paper it serves mainly as a responsive pathway marker elevated by Lr34res-associated defense activation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroflavonols to leucoanthocyanidin-type intermediates in flavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SbDFR3 expression increased after rust inoculation and was more strongly induced in high-Lr34res plants, consistent with enhanced defense flavonoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time-course after P. purpurea inoculation; correlation with pathogen-induced pigmentation and phytoalexin accumulation context.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Contributes precursor formation for downstream anthocyanidin/defense flavonoid products
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: P. purpurea infection; peak induction at 24 hpi
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Pathogen inducible in sorghum leaves, rising at 24 hpi and declining at 48 hpi; ~15-fold induction in high-Lr34res line vs ~5-fold in control.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Same as applied species, endogenous sorghum gene.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study groups SbDFR3 with SbFNR and SbFNSII as part of the early induced pathway converting flavanones toward 3-deoxyanthocyanidin/flavone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Defense flavonoid branch linked to 3-deoxyanthocyanidin/anthocyanidin-related metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NADPH-dependent oxidoreductase of the DFR family
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbDFR3 is induced by rust infection in sorghum. 2. Its induction is stronger in Lr34res transgenic lines, especially high-expression genotypes. 3. This supports enhanced activation of defense flavonoid biosynthesis in Lr34res plants.
